slides.title: Your stomach is *20 minutes behind*.
slides.sub: Fullness hormones take about 20 minutes to reach your brain — so fast eaters blow past full before they feel it.

slides.title: Fullness runs on a timer
slides.body: It takes roughly *20 minutes* for satiety signals to travel from your gut to your brain.

slides.title: Your appetite chemistry
slides.body: As you eat, hunger hormone ghrelin falls and *fullness signals* like leptin and GLP-1 climb — slowly.

slides.title: Fast eating overshoots
slides.body: Finish in five minutes and you'll *eat past full* before the signal ever arrives.

slides.title: Slow the pace down
slides.body: Put the fork down between bites, chew fully, and stretch meals to *at least 20 minutes*.

slides.title: Eat less without trying
slides.body: Give the signal time to land and you naturally stop sooner — *no willpower required*.

slides.title: Appetite control, *on autopilot*.
slides.body: Snap your meals in Vibe Health to spot eating patterns, and let your daily Health Score connect how you eat to how you feel.

caption:
Your fullness hormones take about 20 minutes to reach your brain — so fast eaters blow right past full. 🍽️

Slow down, put the fork between bites, and you eat less without trying. Save this for your next meal. 🔖

Snap your meals in Vibe Health to spot the patterns in your daily Health Score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Eat Slower, Eat Less Without Trying
